text:
# Page 1^page=1
Clarification of Record and Notice of Mischaracterization Prepared by: Cody J. Rice-Velasquez Date: June 28, 2026 Opening Statement Cody J. Rice-Velasquez affirms a deep and ongoing concern for their mother’s well-being and has consistently acted with that priority in mind. However, due to current medical, environmental, financial, logistical, and support-related conditions, the situation has become unstable and unsafe to continue as it has been. Out of necessity, Cody J. Rice-Velasquez has stepped back to protect their own stability and to prevent further harm to either party. Continuing under these conditions would not be responsible, sustainable, or safe. Due to ongoing mischaracterizations of their actions, motives, finances, caregiving role, and character, this structured clarification is provided to ensure an accurate and complete record. This document is not intended as an argument, but as a factual correction based on personal knowledge, direct involvement, and available documentation. 1. Allegation: Cody J. Rice-Velasquez was neglectful or failed in caregiving responsibilities False. Fact: Cody J. Rice-Velasquez provided sustained care, coordination, transportation, relocation support, household setup, cleanup, teardown, pickup, and daily assistance under conditions that exceeded reasonable expectations and lacked adequate structural support. When their mother required assistance with appointments, hospital-related needs, equipment, household organization, moving, setup, or physical logistics, Cody J. Rice-Velasquez was repeatedly the individual performing that work. Much of this work was unpaid, underpaid, or beyond what a single unsupported caregiver could reasonably sustain. The situation became unsustainable because the care burden, coordination burden, financial pressure, and emergency-response responsibilities were concentrated on one individual without a reliable support system. 1

# Page 2^page=2
2. Allegation: Cody J. Rice-Velasquez did nothing, was not present, or does not care False. Fact: The documented actions and contributions of Cody J. Rice-Velasquez directly contradict this claim. They were repeatedly responsible for physical, logistical, and administrative labor, including transporting items, setting up equipment, organizing the home environment, assisting with hospital-related needs, responding to crisis situations, and stabilizing circumstances that others did not manage. Subsequent expressions of concern by others do not negate the labor, time, financial support, transportation, and responsibility already provided. 3. Allegation: Cody J. Rice-Velasquez’s mental health made them incapable of fulfilling the role of caregiver False. Fact: Despite diagnosed conditions, Cody J. Rice-Velasquez maintained responsibility and performance under prolonged stress. Their eventual burnout reflects unsustainable conditions, not incapacity. The demands exceeded what a single unsupported caregiver could reasonably sustain. Any individual, regardless of baseline mental health, would be significantly impacted under similar pressure, instability, lack of support, medical escalation, financial strain, and contradictory demands. The decline in health reflects the failure of the care arrangement, not an inability to function. 4. Allegation: Cody J. Rice-Velasquez abandoned their mother or intended to abandon her False. Fact: There was no premeditated intent to abandon. Cody J. Rice-Velasquez remained actively involved until the situation became unsafe, unstable, and unmanageable. Stepping back constituted a necessary boundary following prolonged instability, escalating accusations, contradictory demands, lack of support, and conditions that impaired functioning. 2

# Page 3^page=3
5. Allegation: Cody J. Rice-Velasquez ignored, blocked, or failed to respond False. Fact: Any gaps in communication were due to logistical constraints, device or technical issues, work obligations, illness, sleep, transportation barriers, or limited access. These were not intentional acts of neglect or avoidance. Delayed responses should not be mischaracterized as neglect or abandonment. 6. Allegation: Cody J. Rice-Velasquez refused to answer the door or denied access False. Fact: Unannounced visits were not always communicated clearly or safely. Cody J. Rice-Velasquez could not reliably hear the front door from certain areas of the home. In some instances, individuals arrived unexpectedly, knocked aggressively, failed to identify themselves, or left before a reasonable response could occur. 7. Allegation: Cody J. Rice-Velasquez took financial advantage or owes money False. Fact: Cody J. Rice-Velasquez provided substantial financial support totaling well over $10,000–$15,000, along with extended access to a vehicle at no cost. Additional contributions included labor, transportation, time, coordination, and caregiving with measurable financial value. Funds provided for specific obligations do not transfer responsibility back if those funds were used for other purposes. Additionally, funds belonging to Cody J. Rice-Velasquez were at times delayed or withheld, impairing their ability to work, generate income, and stabilize the situation. 8. Allegation: Cody J. Rice-Velasquez wrongfully took the Jeep False. 3

# Page 4^page=4
Fact: The Jeep was purchased and paid for by Cody J. Rice-Velasquez and was temporarily titled in their mother’s name for housing-related requirements and practical use. It has always been understood as their vehicle. The mother had use of it at no cost for an extended period. The vehicle remains the property of Cody J. Rice-Velasquez. 9. Allegation: Cody J. Rice-Velasquez pressured their mother to leave subsidized housing False. Fact: The decision to leave subsidized housing was initiated and accelerated by the mother. Cody J. Rice-Velasquez advised caution regarding timing and stability and warned against giving notice before securing alternative housing. After the decision was made, they assisted with the physical and logistical burden. 10. Allegation: Cody J. Rice-Velasquez caused the current housing instability False. Fact: The instability resulted from a combination of decisions, medical needs, lack of support, financial pressure, and rapidly changing circumstances. Cody J. Rice-Velasquez repeatedly attempted to stabilize the situation through transportation, labor, financial support, communication, caregiving, and coordination. 11. Allegation: Cody J. Rice-Velasquez behaved irrationally or inappropriately False. Fact: Any elevated communication occurred within the context of prolonged stress, lack of support, contradictory demands, repeated escalation, and caregiver burnout. Stress responses should not be mischaracterized as irrationality or incapacity. 4

# Page 5^page=5
12. Allegation: Cody J. Rice-Velasquez has a drug problem or acted due to substance abuse False. Fact: This claim is unsupported and inaccurate. The documented issue was caregiver collapse under unsustainable conditions. Any statements implying substance abuse are harmful and do not constitute credible evidence. 13. Allegation: Cody J. Rice-Velasquez should be treated as criminally responsible False. Fact: This is an inflammatory mischaracterization of a caregiving and family crisis. Logistical failures, financial strain, burnout, and inability to meet unrealistic demands do not constitute criminal conduct. 14. Allegation: Cody J. Rice-Velasquez intentionally locked their mother out False. Fact: There was no intentional restriction of access. The issue involved technical problems with the lock, including incorrect code attempts and battery issues, within a broader context of contradictory instructions and instability. 15. Context: Indianapolis Period During the period in Indianapolis, Cody J. Rice-Velasquez faced transportation failures, vehicle issues, technical problems, financial access limitations, work constraints, and communication barriers. Their absence was not voluntary neglect but the result of logistical immobilization during an unstable situation. 5

# Page 6^page=6
16. Context: Hospitalizations and Physical Labor Cody J. Rice-Velasquez handled transportation, equipment movement, setup, teardown, pickup, and household organization during hospitalizations and transitions. These tasks were physically demanding, time-sensitive, and often unpaid. 17. Context: Third-Party Assumptions Many external judgments were based on incomplete or one-sided information. Individuals who did not provide equivalent support should not be considered fully informed regarding the situation. 18. Context: Escalation and Contradictory Demands Expectations and instructions repeatedly shifted, creating instability. Cody J. Rice-Velasquez was alternately relied upon and threatened with removal, police involvement, or sudden changes in responsibility, making consistent caregiving impossible. 19. Boundary Going Forward Cody J. Rice-Velasquez remains concerned for their mother’s well-being but cannot continue as the unsupported default solution. Future planning should be handled through appropriate support systems with clear expectations. Final Statement This document serves as a formal clarification of the record. Cody J. Rice-Velasquez does not consent to continued misrepresentation of their actions, motives, finances, caregiving role, mental health, character, or involvement. Any continued repetition of inaccurate claims should be evaluated in light of this clarification. This record is preserved due to the escalation of the situation and its impact on safety, stability, reputation, finances, and caregiving role. 6

# Page 7^page=7
Supporting documentation may be provided if necessary. 7
